content:
AUDIENCE:
The reader is a curious 8-year-old child who enjoys stories with a bit of spookiness but nothing too scary or gory. They are starting to understand feelings and emotions deeply but might get confused by complex ideas or very dark themes. They care about characters they can relate to or feel sorry for, even if those characters are a little scary. They like stories that have a gentle tone, with a mix of mystery and kindness, and that help them think about why someone might act in a scary way. They might get bored by long, complicated descriptions or stories that are too violent or sad.

===BEGIN===
Hi, I’m Shadow, but don’t be scared of me. I live in the dark forest where no one ever comes. People say I’m a monster because I scare them, but I don’t want to be scary—I just want friends. You see, I’m all alone, and when someone walks near my home, I get so nervous that my shadow grows big and loud. I don’t mean to frighten them; I just want to say hello, but I don’t know how. If I had a friend, maybe I wouldn’t have to be the “bad guy” anymore. So next time you hear a spooky sound in the forest, remember, it might just be me trying to find a friend, not a monster trying to hurt you. Sometimes, even monsters just want to be loved.
